2603.17356.title: PACE-RAG: Patient-Aware Contextual and Evidence-based Policy RAG for Clinical Drug Recommendation
2603.17356.paper_summary: Drug recommendation requires a deep understanding of individual patient context, especially for complex conditions like Parkinson's disease. While LLMs possess broad medical knowledge, they fail to capture the subtle nuances of actual prescribing patterns. Existing RAG methods also struggle with these complexities because guideline-based retrieval remains too generic and similar-patient retrieval often replicates majority patterns without accounting for the unique clinical nuances of individual patients. To bridge this gap, we propose PACE-RAG (Patient-Aware Contextual and Evidence-based Policy RAG), a novel framework designed to synthesize individual patient context with the prescribing tendencies of similar cases. By analyzing treatment patterns tailored to specific clinical signals, PACE-RAG identifies optimal prescriptions and generates an explainable clinical summary. Evaluated on a Parkinson's cohort and the MIMIC-IV benchmark using Llama-3.1-8B and Qwen3-8B, PACE-RAG achieved state-of-the-art performance, reaching F1 scores of 80.84% and 47.22%, respectively. These results validate PACE-RAG as a robust, clinically grounded solution for personalized decision support. Our code is available at: https://github.com/ChaeYoungHuh/PACE-RAG.
2603.17356.paper_summary_zh: 藥物推薦需要深入了解個別病人的背景，特別是對於像帕金森病這樣的複雜病症。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）擁有廣泛的醫學知識，但它們無法捕捉實際處方模式的微妙差異。現有的檢索增強生成（RAG）方法在這些複雜性上也面臨挑戰，因為基於指導方針的檢索仍然過於通用，而類似病人的檢索往往重複大多數模式，卻未考慮到個別病人的獨特臨床細微差異。為了彌補這一空白，我們提出了PACE-RAG（以病人為中心的情境和基於證據的政策RAG），這是一個新穎的框架，旨在將個別病人的背景與類似案例的處方趨勢進行綜合。通過分析針對特定臨床信號的治療模式，PACE-RAG識別最佳處方並生成可解釋的臨床摘要。在帕金森病患者群體和MIMIC-IV基準上使用Llama-3.1-8B和Qwen3-8B進行評估，PACE-RAG達到了最先進的表現，分別達到80.84%和47.22%的F1分數。這些結果驗證了PACE-RAG作為個性化決策支持的穩健、臨床基礎解決方案。我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/ChaeYoungHuh/PACE-RAG。
2603.17356.author: Chaeyoung Huh et.al.
2603.17356.authors: Chaeyoung Huh, Hyunmin Hwang, Jung Hwan Shin, Jinse Park, Jong Chul Ye
